mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 55% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and weak consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate robust residential demand, driving positive equity returns over a quarter through construction and financial sector earnings.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in housing series fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding capacity utilization and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during recovery phases, with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 235.0 signals accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate margins, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation readings above the threshold produce positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of cases, invalidating the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates structural labor market tightening, boosting wage growth and consumer spending to drive positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, directly translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates show no predictive power for 20-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals supply chain contraction and weakening manufacturing activity, causing immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines trigger positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, contradicting the manufacturing slowdown channel.
